description: Small craft built for interplanetary patrol and short-range interception. Their speed, cheapness, and combat utility make them a popular choice as inexpensive system patrol craft for stations, outposts, and budget-conscious belt militias.
The smallest craft that's regularly used for interplanetary transit, a shuttle is a cheap means of moving small amounts of precious material or important persons between worlds.

A hull type much beloved by adventurers, a free merchant can't win a stand-up fight, but it can carry enough Kibble, fuel, and spare parts to keep a crew alive through the long burns between Ceres and Titan.

The hull of choice for DAVE customs cutters and corporate system security, the patrol boat is a light frigate built heavy enough to overawe small merchant vessels while still being relatively cheap to build and crew.

The smallest true combat frigate, and often simply called a "frigate" by spacers. Corvettes have significantly thicker armor than patrol boats and trade additional crew needs and less maneuverability for more available free mass.

The heaviest ship that most poor or resource-depleted stations or belt outposts can build, the heavy frigate can carry a significant loadout of weaponry and has enough crew to overwhelm most pirate ships if it comes to a boarding action.
This class of huge cargo ship is found most often in peaceful, heavily-trafficked inner-system trade lanes. They're slow, soft, and utterly dependent on system peace to survive.

The favored ship of the line of most wealthy, well-armed factions, and often the biggest and most powerful ship most organizations can build. A cruiser's heavy armor and infrastructural support for heavy guns make it a lethal weapon against frigates.
Dreaded hulks of interplanetary war, very few factions have the necessary technology or economy to support the massive expense of building and crewing a battleship.

The queen of a fleet, even fewer polities can afford to build one of these huge ships. Carriers can support flights of fighter or frigate-class warships, ones specially equipped to handle particular missions.
A Bannerjee-12, a common model found on poor or backwater outposts with little traffic. Stations have no Speed score, no interplanetary drive, and cannot perform any maneuvers in combat.
An Arx, a major trade station for a hub world with money to spare. Stations have no Speed score, no interplanetary drive, and cannot perform any maneuvers in combat.
